concerns.text: Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) requires checksums recorded in state/projects/PROJ-492-evaluating-the-statistical-validity-of-p.yaml artifact_hashes map, but T076/T077 write to data/checksums.txt and output/checksums.txt instead. This silently relaxes the constitution's data hygiene requirement.

Constitution Principle VI (Statistical Consistency Verification) requires discrepancies be 'justified in the analysis notebook before results are accepted,' but no task creates or uses an analysis notebook artifact. This drops a constitutionally-mandated verification step.

Constitution Governance specifies Advancement-Evaluator Agent as sole writer of current_stage and invalidates stale records on artifact hash changes, but no task implements this agent's validation workflow. This silently relaxes the constitution's governance requirement.

FR-005b requires sensitivity analysis of baseline proportion across range 0.02–0.10 (step 0.01), but T031 only mentions implementing binomial test and Wilson CI without explicit sensitivity analysis implementation. This weakens the FR deliverable.

SC-001 requires a manually annotated validation set of ≥100 summaries for extraction accuracy measurement, but no task creates, sources, or validates this manual annotation dataset. This drops an acceptance criterion.

FR-028 requires Quickstart guide enabling 30 URLs in ≤30 minutes, but T037 only creates the guide file without task to actually verify the 30-minute execution time claim. The spec should make verification explicit.
T009 and T075 both address deterministic seeding with overlapping scope, creating redundant task coverage. This is a writing issue, not a constraint violation.

Quickstart guide references run_audit.sh and Docker execution, but plan.md shows src/cli/run_audit.py as primary entry point without explicit shell script implementation task. This is a spec inconsistency, not a constraint violation.
